Video.summary: In part two, will accused liar Sarah, who fibbed about having cancer, miscarriage and muscular dystrophy, change her ways?
Video.title: Liar,Liar: Sarah Faces Her Accusers

Video.summary: Demi Moore did it. So did Madonna and Halle Berry. This trend of older women dating younger men is called the Cougar Craze, and it’s sweeping the country. Recently, at a New York speed-dating event, "Sugar Mamas and Boy Toys," good-looking 35-year-old men wooed women over 35 who made at least $500,000 a year. Jeremy organized the event and says he was just answering a demand from affluent ladies. Was he sincere in his matchmaking or just out to make a buck? Dr. Phil talks to two women who participated in the event -- Gail, 44 and Nancy, 50. Their experiences might surprise you. Then, 51-year-old Kat says she’s no cougar; she just likes to date younger guys because men her age are set in their ways. She says her 23-year-old ex-boyfriend, Ryon, was loving, passionate and had a much higher libido than older men. Her friend, Nancy, says Kat has a great head for business, but a bad one when it comes to the opposite sex. She says young men just see dollar signs when they see Kat. Is Kat dating down, or should Nancy just butt out? And, Bobbi had an 11-year relationship with a man who was 17 years her junior, but she says once she turned 50, he saw her as too old. She wants to be in a relationship again, but not with an older man. Her son, Nathan, says someone still wet behind the ears can’t appreciate all Bobbi has to offer. Does Nathan have the right to put his foot down with his mother? Do you think it's OK for older women to date younger men? Take our poll!
Video.title: The Cougar Craze

Video.summary: A man accuses his ex-wife and her new husband of killing his children.
Video.title: Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?

Image.alt: Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?
Mom to Blame for Babies' Death?

Video.summary: Dr. Phil debates viewers who disagree with the advice he gave to previous guests. One woman feels he had no right to push his view of adoption on pregnant 15-year-old, Alexandra. Another woman accuses Dr. Phil of bullying the participants on the Weight Loss Challenge and even accuses him of disliking fat people! What does Dr. Phil say about her inflated accusations? Then a 16-year-old bi-racial girl disputes Dr. Phil’s advice to a mother facing a racial identity crisis. How will these viewers fare when they go head to head with Dr. Phil?
Video.title: Debate Dr. Phil

Video.summary: Jessica says her mother, Patricia, claims she is defying the odds by becoming a record-setting weight lifter while simultaneously battling Stage 3 brain cancer. But Jessica insists her mother isn’t sick at all and that she is lying about her health to gain sympathy and attention. She says Patricia has been spinning stories about various medical ailments for as long as she can remember -- and it’s time to come clean. Patricia strongly denies her daughter’s accusations and insists her diagnosis and incredible survival story are real. Why does she claim Jessica is out to get her? Emotions run high when mother and daughter meet on Dr. Phil’s stage. Do Patricia’s claims hold up? And, can this mother and daughter repair their fractured relationship?
Video.title: Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?

Image.alt: Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?
Why Is My Mom Faking Cancer?

Video.summary: They’re self-absorbed, spoiled and entitled brats, otherwise known as Generation Me. MTV correspondent Kim Stolz recently hit the streets of New York to find out how the younger generation feels about this label. Don't miss the candid responses. Then, Bradley, 24, says he wants to be rich and drive a Lamborghini, but he only wants to put in 80 percent of the effort. He still relies on his mom, Christine, to pay his rent and other bills. Will Bradley learn to stand on his own two feet? Plus, Monica says her 18-year-old daughter, Courtney, is an “entitlement princess,” and she gives the teen money just to keep the peace. Will mom and daughter get a wake-up call? And Jean Twenge, author of Generation Me, reveals why many young people are narcissistic.
Video.title: Generation Me

Video.summary: Meleah and Greg's young love story started and ended like so many. But when Meleah discovered she was pregnant following their breakup, their lives became forever intertwined. Watch as sparks fly when they face off! Plus, Paige was a teen mom struggling to make ends meet. You won't want to miss her remarkable update!
Video.title: Baby After Breakup

Video.summary: Amanda Knox spent four years locked in an Italian prison for the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Dubbed “Foxy Knoxy” by the press, she became one of the most famous cases of wrongful conviction. She tells her story, as she fights to save the life of Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed. This program contains graphic discussion about a criminal case. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Amanda Knox: Freeing The Innocent

Video.summary: Secrets are revealed in a family with a shopaholic wife and rageaholic husband.
Video.title: Family Secrets, Family Lies

Video.summary: A mom is supposed to love and nurture her children, but what if she's too involved? Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling, manipulative and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration, not mourning. He claims she uses bribes to control his every move, including offering him $20,000 to divorce his first wife. Margaret admits that she can be pushy and difficult but says she just wants to see her son succeed in life. Is she really setting him up for failure? Does Reed really want to man-up and break free from his mother's grasp? And, is Margaret willing to let him go? Then, 35-year-old Anthony, star of the new TLC reality show Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home and letting his mom, Patti, wait on him hand and foot. How does Patti feel about their living arrangement? Will she ever kick Anthony out? Plus, hear how Anthony's lifestyle affects his dating life. If you know of a mother-son bond that's a little too close for comfort, don't miss this Dr. Phil. Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration. Margaret says she wants to see her son succeed - but is she setting him up for failure? Plus, 35-year-old Anthony, star of TLC's Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home.
Video.title: Over The Top Moms

Video.summary: Statistics show that 2,000 teens get pregnant every day. Moms and dads: Watch this show with your teenagers, because it can impact their lives and yours! Two families face off over who should raise baby Miley. Paige, 17, and her boyfriend, Jordan, 18, are determined to raise their child on their own, even though they live with Jordan’s dad, Chris. Nikki, Paige’s stepmom, thinks she and her husband should raise the child, because they can better provide for her, and she wants Paige to break up with Jordan. Why does she believe that Jordan is brainwashing Paige? The teen dad says Nikki is the one who’s controlling, and he’s stepped up to the plate. Will the families come to an agreement? See what Dr. Phil tells them they must do to ensure Miley grows up in a healthy environment. Plus, find out the 10 things teens want their parents to know about teen sex.
Video.title: Fight Over Teen's Baby

Video.summary: Melanie says her 19-year-old daughter's boyfriend, Michael, is a gay, drug abusing gangbanger. What would cause her to have such a negative opinion of him? And is there any truth to her allegations? Learn why Michael initially agrees to a drug test, and then refuses. Will he finally break down and take it? And why does Melanie’s daughter, Caylin, support him anyway, regardless of the outcome? Then, Lori says her 17-year-old daughter, Mackenzie, is obsessed with a boy who's already broken up with her four times. Find out why Dr. Phil has a stern message for this mom.
Video.title: Hands Off My Teen Daughter

Video.summary: Ask any teenager, and he or she will tell you how tough life is: schoolwork, dating and dealing with pushy parents. But imagine having two kids while you're still in high school. Dr. Phil sits down with Corey and Kim, a troubled young couple, to see if their relationship can be saved.
Video.title: Troubled Teen Love

Video.summary: When you get married, you look forward to a life filled with love, happiness and security. But what if you find out your spouse has been telling you lies and is someone completely different than the person you thought you knew?
Video.title: Lies And Betrayal

Video.summary: A man's claim of his father's disappearance leads to a shoking decption.
Video.title: Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death

Image.alt: Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death

Video.summary: A mother and father desperate to save their daughter from her self-destructive behavior reach out to Dr. Phil for help. Terri and Bob say their 17-year-old daughter, Daniella, went from a sweet and innocent girl to a troubled and rebellious young woman who skips school, uses drugs and lashes out at them repeatedly. Daniella says she's struggling with her sexual identity and is afraid her parents won't accept her choices. Bob, who admits to using homophobic slurs in the past, says he loves his daughter regardless of her sexuality, and he just wants his little girl back. But did a recent argument tell a different story? What hurtful words pushed Daniella to run away from home? Watch as former Dr. Phil guests Debbie and Brandon track down Daniella and stage an intervention. Can they convince her to sit down with Dr. Phil? Will she get the acceptance she's seeking from her family? Terri and Bob are desperate to save their 17-year-old daughter, Daniella, from her self-destructive behavior. Daniella says she's struggling with her sexual identity and fears her parents won't accept her choices. Can Dr. Phil help this troubled teen?
Video.title: Saving Daniella

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his conversation with Christina, who emailed him desperate for help for her daughter, Tara, who she says can be an angry monster. Christina left the home when Tara was 13 years old, but now, she says she's worried that her daughter is in a toxic situation with the ex-husband she calls narcissistic and his 22-year-old new wife who is 20 weeks pregnant. Today, meet the new wife and find out if Dr. Phil can help this chaotic triangle.
Video.title: Family Triangle Drama (Part 2)

Video.summary: Darcy says that her sister, Jennifer’s, home is in a state of complete chaos. She says Jennifer and her husband, Ralph, have no rules, their kitchen is bare, the electricity is often shut off, and they put their needs ahead of those of their four children -- who are acting out as a result. Darcy says her nephews, 8 and 10, smoke marijuana, play with weapons and recently started a local forest fire -- and that the 10-year-old once wrote a note asking for someone to kill him in his sleep. She says her 12-year-old niece was recently called to truancy court, and her 16-year-old niece is having unprotected sex in the home with her older, live-in boyfriend. Ralph concedes that he’s struggling to provide for his family financially and has made mistakes as a parent, but says Jennifer is to blame, claiming she’s checked out as a mother. Jennifer admits she’s detached from her family, struggles with depression and has trouble maintaining the household. With this family in dire need of a resolution, Dr. Phil stages a no-nonsense intervention. Are Jennifer and Ralph willing to take the necessary steps to save their family?
Video.title: Parenting Disasters

Video.summary: Angelique recounts escaping a life-threatening scheme while pregnant.
Video.title: Barely Escaping a Fetal Abduction

Video.summary: Tiffany says her relationship with her son’s father, Erik, was destroyed by his constant lying and empty promises, to the point where she completely cut him off and has not seen him in over three years. Erik admits he has lied to his ex-girlfriends. Then, just this past year, Erik dated Sheka who says after doing research midway through their relationship, she uncovered his lies and put a stop to their wedding plans. So, when did Erik begin to con and deceive people? As Erik is accused of one lie more shocking than the next, his mom, Jody, says her son has been lying since he was a child. And, don't miss part 2, when more women come forward from Erik’s past.
Video.title: Our Lying Ex-Boyfriend

Video.summary: Em says her husband, Shawn, has cheated on her with at least 78 women -- so why does she keep taking him back? Shawn admits to at least 20 affairs but says it’s his wife’s fault that he strays.
Video.title: Why Men Cheat

Video.summary: Dr. Phil tackles two news stories that have captivated millions. First up, is it ever appropriate to confront your child’s bullies? That’s what James Jones says he did when he boarded his 12-year-old daughter, Chatari’s, school bus, and the video of his tirade went viral! James says some of the students terrorized his child, who suffers from cerebral palsy, and his actions resulted in two second-degree misdemeanor charges against him. CNN contributor Roland Martin joins the show, and he says James should be named Father of the Year for protecting Chatari, who was reportedly hit, called names and had condoms thrown at her. Roland goes head-to-head with attorney Areva Martin, who says James’s behavior was unwarranted. Did this dad cross the line? What does Dr. Phil think? Plus, the disappearance of David Hartley reads like a murder mystery. His wife, Tiffany, says they were on vacation when David was shot in the head by Mexican pirates while jet skiing. His body was never recovered, and Mexican authorities question if the event really occurred. Some people wonder if Tiffany was involved, but she maintains her innocence. Find out why she declined a lie detector test, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for her.
Video.title: Dad's Bus Confrontation

Video.summary: Christina, 21, and her younger sister, Carlie, 13, say they had a close relationship with their father, Michael, until two years ago when he decided to transition to a female. Michael was a police officer, soldier and loving parent who lived his life as a male for 40 years. Now, he wants his daughters to call him Mom, because he says Dad is gone, but they refuse. Michael, who prefers to be called Kayla, says she wants to explain to her daughters why she's living as a woman, but they've never given her the chance. The last time Christina saw her father was two years ago when he showed up at her work in lace and blue jeans to break the news that he's transgendered. When she sees Kayla onstage, does she greet her with open arms? Next, Carlie joins her sister and father. She says she wants her old dad back, but will she feel the same after seeing Kayla? Can the father-daughter bond be turned into a mother-daughter one? Be prepared for an emotional journey as two daughters struggle to understand how someone they loved so much could hurt them so badly.
Video.title: Daddy Drama

Video.summary: Dr. Phil's guests haven't even been married a year and are already wondering if it’s time to call it quits.
Video.title: The Honeymoon’s Over

Video.summary: Terrell Owens is a six-time NFL pro-bowler and arguably one of the best wide receivers to play the game, but he may be better known for his antics off the field. His tumultuous career has been well-documented, and he's been called arrogant, toxic and friendless. The football great sits down with Dr. Phil to set the record straight about the bad rap he says he’s been getting, especially from the mothers of three of his children, who claim he's also a deadbeat dad who is behind on child support payments and makes little attempt to see his kids. What does Terrell think of their accusations? See what happens when the moms come together to confront the alleged absentee dad on Dr. Phil's stage about missed birthday parties, ignored phone calls and empty promises. Is Terrell living in the lap of luxury while his kids go without? What kind of relationship does he want with his children? Dr. Phil delves into Terrell's past to help him create a better future for his children. Plus, at 38, will he play in the NFL again?
Video.title: A Football Star’s Daddy Drama

Video.summary: "I wanted to make someone jealous." "I was bored." "I wanted to feel closer to God." These are just a few of the 237 reasons why women say they have sex. Author and clinical psychologist Dr. Cindy Meston and Dr. Jennifer Berman, a sexual health expert, join Dr. Phil to talk about women and sex, and offer advice to couples with differing libidos.
Video.title: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Image.alt: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Video.summary: Amie says she has no idea how to control her 7-year-old son, Jayden, and even began posting videos of his outbursts online seeking help. But Jayden’s father, Chad, says Amie is just out for attention and blames her for their son’s problems. Can these exes learn to co-parent peacefully?
Video.title: His Ex Posts Son’s Tantrums

Video.summary: Parents say their daughter's home is unsafe for grandson.
Video.title: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Image.alt: Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!
Grandson in a Filthy Death Trap!

Video.summary: Who will Brad choose: his wife or girlfriend?
Video.title: Torn Between 2 Lovers Part 1

Video.summary: As a parent, you're likely to be concerned about sexual predators who could potentially gain access to your child. But what if you suspected a child molester were living under the same roof as your child or grandchild? Dr. Phil tackles the stories of two concerned moms.
Video.title: Is There a Predator in the House?

Image.alt: Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?
Is There a Predator in the House?

Video.summary: When Pierre, his wife, Amanda, and his mother, Yolande, came to see Dr. Phil, the couple complained his mom was a meddling-mother-in-law. Amanda said Yolande never approved of her, and Yolande admitted she wanted her son to be with a more educated woman. Is this family still at odds?
Video.title: Divorce That Daughter-in-Law

Video.summary: Fifty-six-year-old widow Sebrina says she’s in love with her overseas boyfriend of one year, “James,” whom she has talked to online and over the phone but has never met in person. Sebrina admits she has sent “James” more than $250,000 -- emptying her life savings -- but believes he will pay her back, as promised, when he pays off business debt, returns to the United States and marries her. Sebrina’s daughter, Sarah, says she’s at her wits’ end trying to convince her mother that she’s being scammed -- even presenting evidence to prove it -- but Sebrina continues to send “James” money. Dr. Phil enlists the help of private investigator Doug Kane to look into “James’” claims and find the truth. Has Sebrina fallen victim to a catfish? Plus, don’t miss what happens when Dr. Phil confronts “James” over the phone!
Video.title: Hooked By A Catfish
